description: Phase 5 — DDGC Town Meta-Loop, Curio Interaction, and Equipment Systems. Implement the full meta-game cycle: town buildings, curio/trap/obstacle dungeon interactions, trinket/equipment progression, quirk/disease system, and affliction/virtue overstress mechanics.

userStories.title: P5a-0: Define curio, trap, and obstacle data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need data structures for curios, traps, and obstacles so the game layer can represent room and corridor interactions beyond combat.
acceptanceCriteria: CurioDefinition struct in src/interaction/ with: id (String), dungeon_scope (Vec<Dungeon>), results (Vec<CurioResult>), item_interactions (Vec<ItemInteraction>), tags (Vec<String>)
CurioResult struct: weight (u32), chance (f64), result_type (CurioResultType enum: Nothing/Loot/Quirk/Effect/Purge/Scouting/Teleport/Disease), result_id (String)
ItemInteraction struct: item_id (String), overrides_result_id (String)
TrapDefinition struct: id (String), success_effects (Vec<String>), fail_effects (Vec<String>), health_fraction (f64), difficulty_variations (Vec<TrapVariation>)
TrapVariation struct: level (u32), fail_effects (Vec<String>), health_fraction (f64)
ObstacleDefinition struct: id (String), fail_effects (Vec<String>), health_fraction (f64), torchlight_penalty (f64)
All structs derive Serialize/Deserialize/Clone/Debug
Focused test proves struct construction and serde round-trip are deterministic
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Data-model slice only. Match schemas from Curios.csv, Traps.json, Obstacles.json. Place in new src/interaction/ module.

userStories.title: P5a-1: Parse DDGC trap and obstacle definitions from JSON
userStories.description: As a developer, I need DDGC trap and obstacle data parsed from Traps.json and Obstacles.json so actual game content drives hazard interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: A TrapRegistry struct holds TrapDefinition entries parsed from DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Curios/Traps.json
An ObstacleRegistry struct holds ObstacleDefinition entries parsed from DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Curios/Obstacles.json
All 3 trap types (poison_cloud, spikes, blade_wheel) and all 5 obstacle types (thorny_thicket, rubble, shipwreck, ancestor, town_rubble) are parsed
Trap difficulty variations (level 3 and level 5) are preserved with correct fail_effects and health_fraction values
Focused test proves: (a) registry lookup by ID works, (b) trap variation lookup by level works, (c) obstacle ancestor has zero health_fraction and torchlight_penalty
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Traps.json and Obstacles.json are standard JSON with a 'props' array. Parse using serde_json. Source files are in DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Curios/.

userStories.title: P5a-2: Parse DDGC curio definitions from Curios.csv
userStories.description: As a developer, I need DDGC curio data parsed from Curios.csv so room exploration can use actual curio content with weighted result tables and item interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: A CurioRegistry struct holds CurioDefinition entries parsed from DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Curios/Curios.csv
Curios.csv uses a multi-row block format — a custom parser extracts curio blocks with ID, results, item interactions, tags, and dungeon scope
At least 12 curios are parsed (3 per dungeon region: QingLong, BaiHu, ZhuQue, XuanWu)

Focused test proves: (a) registry lookup by ID works, (b) dungeon-scoped curio filtering returns only curios for that dungeon, (c) item interaction overrides are preserved with correct item_id and result_id
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Curios.csv uses a multi-row block format (not standard CSV). Each curio block spans ~10 rows: row 1 = index/name/category, row 3 = id/result types/weights/chances, rows below = region/tags/item interactions. Need a custom line-by-line parser.

userStories.title: P5a-3: Implement curio interaction resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a deterministic curio interaction resolver so room exploration produces outcomes matching DDGC's weighted result system.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_curio_interaction(curio_id, has_item, item_id, seed, registry) returns CurioInteractionOutcome
Without item: selects result from weighted result table using seeded probability (cumulative weight + seed-based selection)
With item: checks item_interactions for a matching item_id override; if found, uses override result_id; otherwise falls back to weighted table
CurioInteractionOutcome struct: result_type (CurioResultType), result_id (String), applied_effects (Vec<String>)

Focused test proves: (a) bare interaction follows weighted probabilities deterministically for a given seed, (b) item interaction produces override result, (c) unknown curio returns a fallback outcome
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DDGC curios have result types like Loot, Quirk, Effect, Disease — the resolver dispatches by type but actual effect application (e.g., applying a quirk) is deferred to the relevant subsystem. Use cumulative-weight selection with seed.

userStories.title: P5a-4: Implement trap and obstacle resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need deterministic trap and obstacle interaction so corridor and room hazards produce correct damage and debuff outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_trap_interaction(trap_id, trap_level, resist_chance, seed, registry) returns TrapOutcome enum (Success with effects, Fail with effects and health_fraction)
Trap difficulty variations are applied: base (level 0), level 3, level 5 — each with potentially different fail_effects and health_fraction
resolve_obstacle_interaction(obstacle_id, registry) returns ObstacleOutcome with fail_effects, health_fraction, and torchlight_penalty

Focused tests prove: (a) trap succeeds when resist roll passes, (b) trap fails with correct difficulty-scaled effects for each level, (c) obstacles apply uniform penalty (5% HP, -20 torchlight except ancestor=0)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Traps have a resist check using seed. Obstacles always apply fail effects. DDGC MapGenerator currently sets hallway trap/obstacle density to 0, but the resolution logic must exist for completeness.

userStories.title: P5a-5: Wire curio/trap/obstacle into room exploration flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need room exploration events to trigger curio, trap, and obstacle interactions so the dungeon run produces the full exploration cycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Room metadata gains optional interaction fields: curio_id (Option<String>), trap_id (Option<String>), obstacle_id (Option<String>)
Run flow: entering a room with a curio produces a CurioInteractionOutcome recorded in the run trace
Run flow: traversing a corridor with a trap produces a TrapOutcome recorded in the run trace
At least one run slice (e.g., a QingLong short variant) produces curio interaction and trap interaction entries in its trace
Existing run slices continue passing without modification
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration slice. Room generation should assign curios/traps based on DungeonMapConfig densities from Phase 4c. Add interaction event types to the run trace (similar to reactive event trace entries).

userStories.title: P5b-0: Define town building and upgrade data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need data structures for town buildings, their upgrade trees, and town state so the game layer can represent the between-run meta state.
acceptanceCriteria: TownBuilding struct in src/town/ with: id (String), building_type (BuildingType enum: Abbey/Blacksmith/StageCoach/Guild/NomadWagon/Sanitarium/Tavern/Garden/LegacyTower/CampingTrainer), unlock_quest_count (u32), upgrade_trees (Vec<UpgradeTree>)
UpgradeTree struct: tree_id (String), levels (Vec<UpgradeLevel>)
UpgradeLevel struct: code (String, a-g), cost (u32), effects (Vec<UpgradeEffect>)
UpgradeEffect enum: SlotCount(u32), StressHealRange(u32, u32), RecruitCount(u32), GoldCost(u32) plus building-specific variants
TownState struct: building_levels (HashMap<String, u32>), gold (u32), heirloom_currencies (HashMap<String, u32>), hero_roster (Vec<HeroRosterSlot>)
All structs derive Serialize/Deserialize/Clone/Debug
Focused test proves TownState construction and upgrade level application are deterministic
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Data-model slice. Place in new src/town/ module. DDGC buildings have variable structures — Abbey has 3 activity sub-trees, StageCoach has 3 parallel upgrade trees. UpgradeEffect must be extensible for building-specific fields.

userStories.title: P5b-1: Parse DDGC core building definitions (StageCoach, Abbey, Guild)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the 3 most critical DDGC building definitions parsed so the town visit cycle can offer stress heal, recruit, and upgrade services.
acceptanceCriteria: A BuildingRegistry struct holds TownBuilding entries parsed from .building.json files
StageCoach building is fully parsed: roster_size_upgrades, number_of_recruits_upgrades, upgraded_recruits_upgrades with all levels (codes a-e)
Abbey building is fully parsed: 3 activities (meditation, prayer, flagellation) each with slot_upgrades, stress_upgrades, side_effects
Guild building is fully parsed with upgrade trees
Focused test proves: (a) building lookup by ID works, (b) StageCoach roster size 9->31 across 5 upgrade levels, (c) Abbey stress heal ranges per activity tier (meditation: 45/56/70)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Source files: DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Buildings/Building/stage_coach.building.json, abbey.building.json, guild.building.json. Each .building.json has a unique structure — use serde_json::Value for building-specific fields and extract known patterns.

userStories.title: P5b-2: Implement town visit cycle with Abbey and StageCoach
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a town visit phase that offers Abbey stress heal and StageCoach recruit services so the meta-game loop (town -> dungeon -> town) is functional.
acceptanceCriteria: TownVisit struct represents a single town phase with: town_state (TownState), available_activities computed from building_levels
perform_stress_heal(hero_index, activity_name) reduces hero stress by the configured range for the current Abbey upgrade level, deducts gold
perform_recruit() generates a new hero from StageCoach using HeroFamilyRegistry, adds to roster if slot available, deducts gold
TownVisit produces a TownVisitTrace with Vec<TownActivity> entries
Focused test proves: (a) Abbey stress heal reduces hero stress by correct range, (b) gold is deducted, (c) StageCoach recruit adds hero to roster, (d) results are deterministic for given state
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Start with Abbey (3 activities: meditation, prayer, flagellation) and StageCoach (recruit). Stress heal ranges by tier: meditation 45/56/70, prayer 55/69/86, flagellation 65/81/100. Gold costs come from upgrade level cost tables.

userStories.title: P5b-3: Wire town visit into run flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the run flow to include town visits between dungeon runs so the full game loop operates headlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: After a dungeon run completes (RunState::Victory or defeat), a town visit phase begins
Town visit reads hero roster (surviving heroes with HP/stress), gold, heirloom currencies from the completed run
Town visit allows Abbey stress heal and StageCoach recruit
Run trace records town activities alongside dungeon run events
A full-loop run slice (town -> dungeon -> town) completes with heroes progressing between runs (stress reduced, XP gained)
Existing run slices continue passing without modification
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration slice. The run loop becomes: initialize -> town_visit -> dungeon_run -> town_visit -> dungeon_run -> ... Add a new scenario function (e.g., run_full_loop_slice) that exercises the full cycle.

userStories.title: P5b-4: Implement hero XP and leveling from Campaign.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need hero experience and leveling so heroes can progress through resolve levels between dungeon runs.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroProgress struct in src/town/ or src/heroes/: xp (u32), hero_level (u32), resolve_level (u32)
LEVEL_THRESHOLD_TABLE constant: [0, 2, 6, 10, 16, 22, 32, 42] from Campaign.json
RESOLVE_THRESHOLD_TABLE constant: [0, 2, 8, 14, 30, 62, 94] from Campaign.json
add_xp(amount) method advances hero_level when xp crosses level thresholds, and resolve_level when xp crosses resolve thresholds
resolve_level method gates dungeon access: hero with resolve_level > dungeon_level cannot enter low-level dungeons (returns false)
Focused test proves: (a) hero starts at level 0/resolve 0, (b) adding 2 XP reaches hero level 1, (c) adding 8 XP reaches resolve level 1, (d) all threshold boundaries produce correct level-ups
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Source: DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Mechanics/Campaign.json. quest_completion_xp_table: [0, 2, 3, 4]. DDGC level and resolve level are separate — both advance based on total XP but use different threshold tables.

userStories.title: P5c-0: Define trinket and equipment data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need data structures for trinkets and equipment upgrades so hero stats can be computed from gear rather than hardcoded values.
acceptanceCriteria: TrinketDefinition struct in src/equipment/: id (String), buffs (Vec<String>), hero_class_requirements (Vec<String>), rarity (TrinketRarity enum), price (u32), limit (u32), origin_dungeon (String)
TrinketRarity enum: VeryCommon, Common, Uncommon, Rare, VeryRare, Collector, Ancestral, DarkestDungeon, Trophy, Kickstarter + other DDGC tiers
EquipmentDefinition struct: id (String), hero_class_id (String), slot (EquipmentSlot enum: Weapon/Armor), upgrade_level (u32), stat_modifiers (Vec<AttributeModifier>)
AttributeModifier struct: attribute_key (String), value (f64)
All structs derive Serialize/Deserialize/Clone/Debug
Focused test proves construction and lookup are deterministic
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Data-model slice. Place in new src/equipment/ module. Trinket buffs reference JsonBuffs.json buff IDs that map to AttributeModifier entries.

userStories.title: P5c-1: Parse DDGC trinket definitions from JsonTrinkets.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need DDGC trinket data parsed so the loot and shop systems can use actual trinket content.
acceptanceCriteria: A TrinketRegistry struct holds TrinketDefinition entries parsed from DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/JsonTrinkets.json
Trinkets are indexed by ID and have working lookup by ID
At least 10 trinkets across multiple rarity tiers (common, uncommon, rare, very_rare) are parsed and verified with correct buffs, class requirements, and price
Class-requirement filtering: trinkets_for_class(class_id) returns only trinkets whose hero_class_requirements include the given class_id or are empty (no restriction)
Focused test proves: (a) registry lookup returns correct trinket, (b) class filtering works for hunter/diviner/tank, (c) rarity categorization matches source data
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Source: DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/JsonTrinkets.json. Format: {props: [{id, buffs, hero_class_requirements, rarity, price, limit, origin_dungeon}]}. Parse using serde_json.

userStories.title: P5c-2: Implement BuffRegistry for buff-ID-to-modifier resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need trinket/quirk buff references resolved to concrete attribute modifiers so equipped trinkets and acquired quirks modify hero stats correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: A BuffRegistry struct maps buff IDs (String) to Vec<AttributeModifier>

BuffRegistry parses DDGC buff naming conventions: MAXHP-15 -> {key: ATTR_MAX_HEALTH, value: -0.15}, DMGL-25 -> {key: ATTR_ATTACK, value: -0.25}, GIVE_STRESSDMG20 -> {key: stress_damage_given, value: 0.20}
resolve_buffs(buff_ids) takes a Vec<String> and returns aggregated Vec<AttributeModifier> for all resolved buffs
At least 10 buff IDs are registered with correct attribute key and value mappings

Focused test proves: (a) positive and negative modifiers resolve correctly, (b) percentage-based and flat modifiers are distinguished, (c) multi-buff trinkets aggregate all modifiers, (d) unknown buff IDs are skipped with no panic
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DDGC buff IDs use mixed naming conventions. Key patterns: MAXHP +/-N -> max HP percentage, DMGL/DMGH +/-N -> damage low/high percentage, STRESSDMG +/-N -> stress damage, RESIST +/-N -> resistance, SPD +/-N -> speed, etc. Build a lookup table + pattern parser for known conventions.

userStories.title: P5c-3: Parse hero equipment upgrade data and compute stats from gear
userStories.description: As a developer, I need hero combat stats computed from equipment level instead of hardcoded values so the equipment progression system changes hero power.
acceptanceCriteria: EquipmentUpgradeData struct per hero class: weapon_levels (Vec<EquipmentLevel>), armor_levels (Vec<EquipmentLevel>)
EquipmentLevel struct: upgrade_level (u32), stat_modifiers (Vec<AttributeModifier>)
Parse at least 2 hero classes' upgrade data (e.g., Hunter, Tank) from Upgrades/Heroes/*.upgrades.json files
compute_hero_stats(hero_class_id, weapon_level, armor_level, trinket_buffs) returns HashMap<String, f64> of attribute values
Level-0 weapon + level-0 armor + no trinkets produces same stats as current hardcoded values (backward compatibility for Tank: HP 192, ATK 31, SPD 7; Hunter: HP 152, ATK 40, SPD 6)
Focused test proves: (a) level 0 = current stats, (b) level 1 weapon has higher ATK than level 0, (c) trinket buffs modify the computed stats
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Source: DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Upgrades/Heroes/*.upgrades.json. Current hardcoded hero stats in src/content/heroes/ are level-0 values. Parse upgrade trees to get stat modifiers per level. The compute function combines weapon + armor + trinket modifiers.

userStories.title: P5c-4: Wire equipment into hero archetype creation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need hero archetype creation to accept equipment parameters so the same hero class can have different power levels based on gear.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero archetype creation functions in src/content/heroes/ accept optional (weapon_level, armor_level) parameters (default: 0, 0 for backward compatibility)
When equipment parameters are provided, compute_hero_stats is used instead of hardcoded values
All existing tests continue passing without modification (default level-0 equipment)
At least one new test proves a level-1 equipped hero has different ATK stat than level-0
Trinket equip is a separate step: equip_trinket(hero, trinket_id) applies buff modifiers on top of equipment stats
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration slice. All existing archetype creation calls use default (0,0) — no changes needed in existing code. Add optional parameters or builder methods for equipment levels.

userStories.title: P5d-0: Define quirk data model and parse DDGC quirks from JsonQuirks.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need quirk definitions parsed from JsonQuirks.json so heroes can acquire and be affected by quirks and diseases.
acceptanceCriteria: QuirkDefinition struct in src/quirk/: id (String), is_positive (bool), is_disease (bool), classification (String), buffs (Vec<String>), incompatible_quirks (Vec<String>), curio_tag (String), curio_tag_chance (f64)
A QuirkRegistry struct holds QuirkDefinition entries parsed from DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Mechanics/JsonQuirks.json
At least 10 quirks are parsed: mix of positive, negative, and disease types
Quirk buffs are resolvable through BuffRegistry from US-914 (cross-reference, no reimplementation)
Focused test proves: (a) quirk lookup by ID works, (b) positive/negative/disease classification is correct, (c) buff IDs are preserved for later resolution
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Source: DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Mechanics/JsonQuirks.json. Format: {props: [{id, is_positive, is_disease, classification, incompatible_quirks, curio_tag, curio_tag_chance, buffs}]}. Quirks use the same buff ID system as trinkets.

userStories.title: P5d-1: Implement quirk application and HeroQuirkState
userStories.description: As a developer, I need heroes to carry active quirks that modify their attributes so the quirk system affects gameplay.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroQuirkState struct: positive_quirks (Vec<String>), negative_quirks (Vec<String>), diseases (Vec<String>), max_positive (5), max_negative (5)

apply_quirk(state, quirk_id, registry) adds the quirk to the correct category, enforcing: (a) incompatible quirk replacement (check incompatible_quirks list), (b) max slot limit with random replacement when full
resolve_quirk_modifiers(state, buff_registry) returns aggregated Vec<AttributeModifier> from all active quirks' buff IDs
Quirk modifiers integrate into compute_hero_stats alongside equipment and trinket modifiers
Focused test proves: (a) quirk adds modifier to hero stats, (b) incompatible quirk replaces existing quirk, (c) disease quirks tracked in diseases vec not negative_quirks, (d) max slot limit enforced
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DDGC limits heroes to 5 positive and 5 negative quirks. When applying a new quirk and the category is full, randomly replace one existing quirk in that category. Diseases are tracked separately from negative quirks but count against the negative limit.

userStories.title: P5d-2: Wire quirk acquisition into curio and combat events
userStories.description: As a developer, I need quirks to be acquired from curio interactions and combat events so the hero's quirk profile evolves during runs.
acceptanceCriteria: CurioInteractionOutcome result_type Quirk triggers apply_quirk with the result's result_id as quirk_id
CurioInteractionOutcome result_type Disease triggers apply_quirk with the result's result_id as disease quirk_id
Combat disease effects (monster skills with .disease) trigger apply_quirk after combat resolution
At least one end-to-end test proves: (a) curio interaction adds a quirk to a hero, (b) disease from combat is applied, (c) quirk modifiers affect subsequent combat stats
Existing tests continue passing
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration slice connecting curio system (US-904), combat system, and quirk system (US-918). Curio result dispatch happens in the room exploration flow. Combat disease dispatch happens in the battle loop post-resolution.

userStories.title: P5e-0: Define trait/affliction data model and parse from JsonTraits.json
userStories.description: As a developer, I need trait (affliction/virtue) definitions parsed from JsonTraits.json so the overstress system can resolve correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: TraitDefinition struct in src/stress/: id (String), overstress_type (OverstressType enum: Affliction/Virtue), buff_ids (Vec<String>), combat_start_turn_act_outs (Vec<ActOutEntry>), reaction_act_outs (Vec<ReactionEntry>)

ActOutEntry struct: action (ActOutAction enum: Nothing/BarkStress/ChangePosition/IgnoreCommand/RandomCommand/RetreatFromCombat/AttackFriendly/AttackSelf/MarkSelf/StressHealSelf/StressHealParty/BuffRandomPartyMember/BuffParty/HealSelf), weight (u32)
ReactionEntry struct: trigger (ReactionTrigger enum), probability (f64), effect (Option<String>)
A TraitRegistry holds TraitDefinition entries parsed from DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Mechanics/JsonTraits.json
At least 2 afflictions (e.g., fearful, hopeless) and 1 virtue (e.g., courageous) are parsed with correct act-out weight tables and reaction probabilities
Focused test proves: (a) trait lookup by ID, (b) act-out weight tables are preserved, (c) reaction probabilities are preserved
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Source: DreamDeveloperGame-Crossover/Assets/Resources/Data/Mechanics/JsonTraits.json. Format: {props: [{id, overstress_type, curio_tag, buff_ids, combat_start_turn_act_outs, reaction_act_outs}]}. Act-out format: {action: weight}. Parse carefully — nested arrays.

userStories.title: P5e-1: Implement overstress resolution (affliction/virtue roll)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the overstress resolution implemented so heroes who exceed max stress get an affliction or virtue instead of being stuck at max stress.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_overstress(hero_id, seed, trait_registry) rolls between affliction (~90-95% weighted) and virtue (~5-10%) using seeded RNG
Affliction selection uses weighted random from all available affliction TraitDefinitions in the registry
The selected trait is returned as OverstressOutcome { trait_id, overstress_type, buff_ids }
Trait buffs modify hero attributes through the same modifier pipeline (BuffRegistry + compute_hero_stats)

Focused test proves: (a) overstress produces an affliction with high probability across 100 seeded rolls, (b) virtue is possible but rare (at least 1 virtue in 100 rolls), (c) result is deterministic for a given seed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DDGC's virtue chance is approximately 5-10%. Use seeded RNG. When a hero's stress exceeds ATTR_MAX_STRESS (typically 200), this function is called. The result applies the trait's buff_ids as attribute modifiers to the hero.

userStories.title: P5e-2: Implement affliction combat act-outs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need affliction combat behavior (act-outs) implemented so afflicted heroes occasionally disobey orders during combat.
acceptanceCriteria: resolve_act_out(affliction_id, seed, trait_registry) selects an action from the trait's combat_start_turn_act_outs weighted table using seeded RNG
ActOutResult enum: Obey (do nothing, hero acts normally), Disobey(ActOutAction) with the selected disruptive action
Disobey actions: IgnoreCommand (skip turn), ChangePosition (random move), BarkStress (stress ally), AttackFriendly, MarkSelf
Act-out result is recorded in the battle trace with the hero ID and action taken

Focused test proves: (a) Nothing/Obey is the most common outcome for fearful (weight 6 of ~10), (b) IgnoreCommand and BarkStress outcomes are possible, (c) resolution is deterministic for a given seed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: DDGC act-out weights make 'nothing' dominant (weight ~6 out of ~10 total). Other actions have smaller weights (1 each). Use cumulative-weight selection with the seed.

userStories.title: P5e-3: Wire overstress and act-outs into battle loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the battle loop to check for overstress and resolve act-outs so the stress mechanic produces consequences during combat.
acceptanceCriteria: When a hero's stress (ATTR_STRESS) exceeds ATTR_MAX_STRESS, resolve_overstress is called and the resulting trait is applied
At the start of an afflicted hero's turn, resolve_act_out is called; if Disobey, the hero's commanded action is replaced or skipped
Battle trace records overstress events with the resulting trait_id and act-out events with the action taken
At least one end-to-end test proves: (a) stress damage pushes hero over threshold, (b) affliction is applied, (c) act-out occurs on a subsequent turn for that hero
Existing battle tests continue passing (no hero reaches max stress in existing fixtures)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Integration slice. This completes the stress -> affliction -> combat behavior chain. Add a test fixture that applies heavy stress damage to trigger overstress. Ensure existing tests are unaffected (heroes don't reach max stress in normal scenarios).
